stories.headline: US and Iran Agree to Deal Halting War
stories.summary: The US and Iran reached an interim agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, halting a war that killed thousands of people and setting the stage for negotiations on the fate of Iran's nuclear program. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Iran and US agree deal to open Strait of Hormuz and extend ceasefire
stories.summary: Leaders to sign agreement on Friday for reopening of waterway and end to US naval blockade of Iranian ports

stories.headline: Switzerland Rejects Proposal to Cap Population at 10 Million
stories.summary: Switzerland rejected a proposal to cap its population at 10 million people, as warnings about economic damage if the radical measure was introduced outweighed concerns about immigration.

stories.headline: Strait of Hormuz backlog could take weeks to clear after US-Iran deal
stories.summary: Analysts warn oil flows may be slow to recover and remain vulnerable to renewed disruption

stories.headline: Oil Slumps as US-Iran Deal May Allow Strait of Hormuz to Reopen
stories.summary: Oil tumbled after the US and Iran agreed to a peace deal to end the war in the Middle East, potentially allowing the Strait of Hormuz to reopen.

stories.headline: Japanese Stocks Set to Rise After US-Iran Peace Deal Reached
stories.summary: Japanese stocks are set to gain after President Donald Trump said the US and Iran have reached a peace deal, raising hopes the Middle East war is nearing an end and the Strait of Hormuz will reopen.

stories.headline: US-Iran ‘peace deal’ announced, Trump says Strait of Hormuz reopening
stories.summary: Trump announces that a ceasefire deal with Iran has been agreed, and that the Strait of Hormuz will reopen for shipping.

stories.headline: Thousands protest G7 in Geneva as Trump, other leaders ready for summit in France
stories.summary: According to figures from Geneva police, an estimated 20,000 people, including some 600 so-called “Black Bloc” militants, took part in the demonstration.

stories.headline: Iran warns of 'imminent' response as Israel strikes Beirut
stories.summary: Iran warns of imminent retaliation after fresh Israeli strikes kill five in southern Lebanon, as Trump publicly slams Netanyahu over the timing.

stories.headline: Swiss voters reject 10 million population cap
stories.summary: Nearly 55% of participants voted against the proposal to cap population by cutting migration, results show.

stories.headline: Gaza mourns four killed in Israeli strike
stories.summary: Cries of grief echoed through Gaza City for four Palestinians killed near the Jabalia refugee camp in northern Gaza.

stories.headline: Trump stages show of political domination with UFC bout on White House lawn
stories.summary: US president hosts a Maga mixed martial arts spectacle inside a temporary Colosseum on his 80th birthday

stories.headline: Swiss voters reject proposal to cap population at 10mn
stories.summary: Projections showed 54 per cent of voters rejected the initiative backed by the rightwing Swiss People’s party

stories.headline: Trump Says Hormuz Strait to Open Friday Upon Deal Signing
stories.summary: President Trump says the Strait of Hormuz will be “opening” on Friday upon the signing of the deal with Iran.

stories.headline: Macron and Trump test their bruised bromance at G7 summit
stories.summary: French president will host world leaders at Évian-les-Bains from Monday

stories.headline: How to Watch the U.F.C. 250 Freedom Fights at the White House
stories.summary: Seven bouts will take place in an arena created on the South Lawn for the Ultimate Fighting Championship event, which coincides with President Trump’s 80th birthday.

stories.headline: Swiss voters reject proposal to cap population at 10 million
stories.summary: Far-right plan would have obliged the Swiss government to limit the population, currently 9.1 million, to 10 million by 2050Voters in Switzerland have rejected an unprecedented far-right proposal to cap the country’s population at 10 million in a divisive referendum dubbed “the Swiss Brexit”.Some 54.79% of voters were against the proposal by the Swiss People’s party (SVP) and 45.21% were in favour. Turnout was 58.86%. Continue reading...

stories.headline: Live updates: Trump’s UFC fights to kick off at White House as thunderstorms loom
stories.summary: The South Lawn event is meant to celebrate the nation’s 250th anniversary and Trump’s own 80th birthday.

stories.headline: Swiss right-wing plan to cap population rejected by narrow margins
stories.summary: Swiss voters reject a proposed population cap that would cap the country’s population at 10 million.

stories.headline: Thousands protest as Trump, other world leaders set to meet for G7 summit
stories.summary: Activists rally in Geneva to denounce policies of G7 countries ahead of group's annual meeting this week in France.

stories.headline: Swiss voters reject bid to cap population at 10 million, a move that would have limited immigration
stories.summary: A “yes” vote would have required the Swiss government to take action to cap the population by 2050, including limiting immigration.

stories.headline: Trump says US-Iran peace deal 'complete,' to be signed on Friday
stories.summary: Tehran has since confirmed that the "text of the memorandum of understanding" has been agreed on and is to be signed in Switzerland, opening the door to 60-day negotiations "following verification of US commitments," including the lifting of the US blockade.

stories.headline: Protesters clash with police ahead of G7 summit in Geneva
stories.summary: Swiss police deployed a water cannon and fired tear gas to disperse anti-G7 protesters after clashes.

stories.headline: US kills leader of Venezuela's Tren de Aragua gang in air strike, Trump says
stories.summary: Niño Guerrero was killed in a "swift and lethal kinetic strike," the US President wrote in a social media post.

stories.headline: What is in the US-Iran deal?
stories.summary: Agreement will extend ceasefire by 60 days, reopen the Strait of Hormuz and begin nuclear negotiations

stories.headline: US Futures Climb, Oil Falls on Iran Peace Deal: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: US equity futures climbed, oil slumped and the dollar fell after the US and Iran said they had reached a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: UK, France, Germany Ready to Lift Relevant Iran Sanctions
stories.summary: “We are prepared to lift relevant sanctions in response to clear, verifiable steps by Iran on its nuclear programme,” according to a joint statement from the leaders of the UK, France, Germany and Italy.

stories.headline: LIVE: Ilia Topuria vs Justin Gaethje title fight at Trump’s UFC Freedom 250
stories.summary: Title fights, Topuria-Gaethje and Pereira-Gane, to be staged in front of US President Donald Trump at the White House.

stories.headline: Romanian president nominates Adrian Vestea as prime minister
stories.summary: Romania has been embroiled in a renewed political crisis since its government collapsed in May following a no-confidence vote against Liberal Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan.

stories.headline: Deadly overnight Russian attack in Ukraine as industrial facilities were struck deep into Russia
stories.summary: Two killed and four injured in overnight Russian attacks on Ukraine's Sumy Oblast. On the same night, Ukrainian strikes hit strategic Russian industrial facilities located 700km from the border with Ukraine.

stories.headline: Israeli military says Hezbollah targets struck in Beirut
stories.summary: Israel says it targeted the Iran-backed armed group Hezbollah, as Tehran warns this could derail a US-Iran deal to end the fighting.

stories.headline: Trump Owns Shares in Company Hosting U.F.C. Bout as Part of His Vast Holdings
stories.summary: The Trump Organization says the president has no control over his stock positions, including that of TKO Group, which runs the Ultimate Fighting Championship.

stories.headline: Violence erupts during anti-G7 protests in Geneva on eve of summit

stories.headline: Why the US economy keeps defying the odds
stories.summary: Why has the American economy continued to outperform so many of its peers, despite facing the same global shocks?

stories.headline: Stock Market Moves On From SpaceX to Focus On Iran Deal, Fed
stories.summary: After SpaceX and its bankers pulled off the largest-ever IPO without a hitch last week, investors are returning their focus back to Earth.

stories.headline: Bond Funds Chase Australian Debt on Peak RBA Interest Rate Bets
stories.summary: Local and global bond funds are snapping up Australian debt as economic strains signal the end of the Reserve Bank’s hiking cycle.

stories.headline: Japan’s Top Bond-Trading Regional Bank Buys JGBs After Decade
stories.summary: The Japanese regional bank with the best recent record for trading bonds has started buying Japanese government debt for the first time in a decade.

stories.headline: Hong Kong Issuers Seek to Boost Trading in Overlooked Stocks
stories.summary: Hong Kong stock issuers are looking to boost trading in some stocks suffering from thin volumes, which they say is hurting the ability of companies to access financing through follow-on offerings.

stories.headline: Global Funds Retreat From Japan’s Long Bonds as BOJ Goes Slow
stories.summary: Little more than a year since Japan finally offered yields high enough to lure global bond managers back to its debt market, many are starting to retreat.

stories.headline: Lazard Undercuts Rival Centerview in Battle for Venezuela Deal
stories.summary: Investment bank Lazard Inc. is making a late bid to dislodge rival Centerview Partners as Venezuela’s financial adviser, offering to oversee one of the largest-ever sovereign debt restructurings for a sharply lower fee.

stories.headline: SpaceX and OpenAI Are Ending Wall Street’s Era of Stock Scarcity
stories.summary: The stock market is starting to expand again after years of shrinking — and potentially at a scale not seen since the dot-com era.

stories.headline: Bloomberg This Weekend 6/14/2026
stories.summary: The news doesn’t stop when markets close. Hosts David Gura, Christina Ruffini and Lisa Mateo bring clarity, context and a bit of humor to the weekend’s biggest headlines, LIVE from New York. Joined by Sportico Deputy Editor Eben Novy- Williams, Fmr. Deputy National Security Advisor Jon Finer, New York Times Opinion Columnist Nicholas Kristkof, Maryland Rep. Johnny Olszewski, USA Today White House Correspondent Francesca Chambers, Associated Press

stories.headline: Russian families use AI to 'resurrect' loved ones killed in Ukraine
stories.summary: The highly controversial trend lies at the intersection of Russia's war on Ukraine, new AI technologies and grief.

stories.headline: Vincent's parents 'never say he's good enough' - so he turned to a middle-aged couple online

stories.headline: As Trump turns 80, what's it really like to work as an octogenarian?
stories.summary: As President Donald Trump celebrates his birthday, we ask people who have worked into their 80s themselves about what it takes to keep going.

stories.summary: BBC speaks to students from war-torn Myanmar who say they were cheated after paying to study abroad.

stories.headline: Why the economics make this the craziest World Cup ever
stories.summary: From trade wars to soaring ticket prices, the 2026 World Cup is unlike any before it. Faisal Islam explores what this tournament reveals about our changing global economy.

stories.headline: Zelensky under pressure to end row with Poland over WW2 name of army unit
stories.summary: Poland is threatening to strip Ukraine's president of a state honour over the naming Ukrainian military unit after controversial World War Two fighters.

stories.headline: Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon kill 17, reports say
stories.summary: Nine of them were killed in a series of attacks in the town of Tayr Debba, according to Lebanon's state news agency.

stories.headline: Ukraine says missiles hit military plant deep inside Russia

stories.headline: Iran and Israel say they will pause strikes but warn of retaliation if ceasefire breached again
stories.summary: Israel's PM says his country is holding fire "at the moment", after Iran's armed forces said they had stopped military action.

stories.headline: Peru election result close as vote counting continues
stories.summary: The race between right-wing Keiko Fujimori and left-wing Roberto Sánchez has been dominated by concerns over crime and political instability.

stories.summary: In a rare interview, a senior executive at TSMC discusses the AI boom, the geopolitics of chips and what it means for the price of electronics.

stories.headline: 'No dead ends': What the Dutch can teach us about tackling youth unemployment
stories.summary: The Netherlands has one of the world's lowest rates of 16 to 24-year-olds not in education, employment or training.
